content: Skeletonization/medial-axis pitfalls for width measurement: the medial axis is highly sensitive to boundary noise — small jagged/noisy contours on an otherwise straight object produce spurious branches and a noisy centerline, which is a well-known limitation of discrete skeletonization approximations. For curved objects the true medial axis is a 2D/3D surface, not a clean 1D curve; a curvilinear (1-pixel-wide) skeleton is only obtained by pruning, which introduces its own artifacts (skeleton endpoint spurs, especially at mask endcaps). scikit-image's medial_axis(return_distance=True) gives the local width estimate directly at each skeleton pixel via the distance transform, which is the standard trick to get local width without separate perpendicular-ray casting.
